content: We can reframe the basic Self-Refine framework to embed multiple inconsistent solving perspectives drawn from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, turning single-threaded iterative self-feedback into multi-view self-refinement that still retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no extra training or labeled data. This hybrid approach lets a base LLM challenge its own output from disjoint angles at each iteration of refinement, rather than only revising it along its original logical path.
